name: kanbantic-claude-plugin
description: Kanbantic issue lifecycle skills: intake (report / request / propose), triage, prepare, execute, review — all artifacts via Kanbantic MCP. v2.10.0 completes the E066 version-aware plugin track (KBT-F318/F319/F320): the release concept is renamed to Version end-to-end, the lane-skills carry version-flow tools (mcp__kanbantic__ prefix restored), and known-mcp-tools.json is synced to the LIVE registry — the four legacy release-tools (create_release/list_releases/update_release/get_release_notes) are removed and the 12 live Version-flow tools are added (create_version, list_versions, update_version, freeze_version, mark_version_released, preview_next_version, get_version_notes, app_version_at_date, issue_version_lookup, version_audit_timeline, evaluate_rollout_readiness, record_rollout_decision). lint-skills.js gains Invariant 5: any stale release-domain token (releaseId / release_id / capital-cased Release / a removed release-tool) in a lane-skill fails CI unless the line carries the `lint-skills-allow-release` opt-out marker. Two new zero-dependency Node hooks: a PreToolUse `pre-tool-use-locked-version-blocker` that blocks claim_issue when the issue's delivered-in Version is locked-on-deploy (lifecycleStatus ≥ StagingDeployed, KBT-F458) — fail-open on any infra error so it never wedges a session — and a Stop `stop-version-summary` that prints a one-line Version summary from the proxy-maintained session-file. This is the meta-release: the plugin is the first Application published through the new Version-flow (Version v2.10.0 → prod-deploy → F6-handler auto-creates the GitHub Release, KBT-TC2368). See RELEASE_NOTES_v2.10.0.md. v2.9.0 enforces process rules A–E in lane-skills (KBT-F449 / KBT-E084): (1) prepare now declares a per-issue frozen test-policy Decision-entry before transitioning to Prepared — 5F.5 for Features, 5B.6 for Bugs — with N.v.t. requiring ≥20-char rationale and at least one level remaining Vereist; (2) a HARD-GATE in 5E.4 blocks create_test_case on Epics (Regel A: test cases belong to child Features); (3) execute Step 3c loads the frozen policy and enforces it as read-only, defaulting to Vereist/min=1 if missing; (4) execute Step 7 now checks Passed count ≥ min per Vereist level — zero test cases fails coverage even when no test case has status Failed (missing coverage = blocker, not just failing tests); (5) review Step 1b loads discussion entries to parse the frozen policy and count actual Passed per level; (6) reviewer-prompt item 5 always REJECTs for missing coverage on any Vereist level. KBT-TRUL013 is superseded and archived. 18 automated tests cover all paths. See RELEASE_NOTES_v2.9.0.md. v2.8.0 adds the /kanbantic-graduation skill (KBT-E085 / KBT-F431): graduates a concept from the Kanbantic Toolkit into a fully built, reviewed, and merged implementation by orchestrating the full triage → prepare → execute → review pipeline in one command. Seven-step workflow with a 5-criteria ripeness HARD-GATE (KBT-TRUL018) and KBT-RL123-formatted derived test cases. v2.7.0 ships a zero-dependency git credential helper (KBT-B330): the lane-skills (execute / review / prepare) no longer clone with the PAT embedded in the URL — which persisted the plaintext secret into .git/config — and instead configure `credential.helper` so git fetches the token just-in-time from Kanbantic's get_repository_credential over stdin. The PAT never lands in .git/config, a remote URL, shell history, the process list, or the agent transcript (the helper, not the agent, calls get_repository_credential). See RELEASE_NOTES_v2.7.0.md. v2.6.0 adds a readiness-gate override governance flag in the stdio proxy (KBT-F341 / KBT-E069): a successful update_issue_status / claim_issue carrying an overrideReason now triggers a greppable [override-governance] Comment on the affected issue for second-party review. Transparent-bridge preserved (response forwarded verbatim; flag is a fire-and-forget side-effect); opt out with KANBANTIC_SKIP_OVERRIDE_FLAG=1. Companion server-side preventive controls tracked under KBT-E069. v2.5.1 narrows /kanbantic-sync-workspace-skills materialization to Skill+Subagent only (KBT-B250 / KBT-BD086): Command toolkit-items are reference-only snippets and intentionally stay Toolkit-only — v2.5.0 silently exposed them as slash-commands under .claude/commands/, polluting the namespace. v2.5.0 adds /kanbantic-sync-workspace-skills (KBT-F265 / KBT-TRUL014): materializes the active workspace's Toolkit Skill / Subagent items as on-disk `.claude/commands/*.md` and `.claude/agents/*.md` mirrors with a `.kanbantic-sync.json` manifest for drift detection. Companion to Kanbantic API change KBT-F264 (bootstrap_agent now also returns skills/commands/subagents/customs arrays). v2.4.2 patches lane-skill SKILL.md mechanical inconsistencies (KBT-B192 / KBT-RL064). v2.4.1 adds a real-proxy regression test + bundle ↔ live MCP-registry drift detector (KBT-B200). v2.4.0 introduces Phase-of-Features-of-Tasks Epic shape (KBT-F250). v2.3.0 introduced InDeployment (KBT-F236), v2.2.0 introduced Prepared (KBT-F235). See RELEASE_NOTES_v2.6.0.md.
